calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Yang appeared by zoom for and with Barbara Barnes by telephone.

The Court questions why Ms. Barnes has not filed a creditor's claim for reimbursement of the funeral expenses. Mr. Yang stated that the burial was in January 2018, that all of the funeral expenses were paid by the Administrator, that none of the Beneficiaries had money and that Barbara was the only one who could pay for the funeral expenses. Mr. Yang also stated that there is no claim by the funeral home and that his Client is seeking reimbursement. The Court noted that Ms. Barnes paid for the funeral expenses before she was appointed as the Administrator. Mr. Yang agreed that Ms. Barnes paid it before being appointed and stated that at that time, she was acting as the Successor Trustee. The Court instructed Mr. Yang to have his Client file a creditor's claim and get consents from all of the Beneficiaries. Mr. Yang stated that consents were signed and filed by all of the Beneficiaries as an attachment to the petition that was filed on July 2, 2019. The Court informed Mr. Yang that it typically does not reimburse Attorneys for the costs of local trips/travel to Court, for postage, for copying or for faxing, stated that those costs are absorbed as part of the statutory fees and that it will not allow payment for Huber. Mr. Yang stated that he reviewed the Probate Examiner's notes and the local rules regarding expenses, and that he has no issue with the Court not allowing reimbursement for Huber, but he feels duplication (copy) costs and postage are bonified expenses. The Amended First and Final Account and Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To List And Sell Real Property; To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Agent; And Petition To Confirm Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Yang for and with Barbara Barnes (Petitioner).

The Court stated that its understanding is that the Personal Representative has authority to enter into an exclusive listing and sell the property. Mr. Yang agreed and stated that the focus of the petition is for the Court to confirm the proposed Beneficiaries, that the Petitioner is going to file a notice of proposed action, that he is fine with the order just confirming the Beneficiaries and that he will follow the code regarding the rest of the requests in the petition. The Petition for Authority to List and Sell Real Property; to Enter Into an Exclusive Listing Agreement with Real Estate Agent and Petition to Confirm Beneficiaries is granted as the Beneficiaries and denied as to the rest of the petition (subject to the proposed order being reviewed and modified). The Court informed Mr. Yang and Ms. Barnes that no distributions can be made without a petition for final distribution.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
